# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template for an S3 bucket that stores user-uploaded files and application backups. Allow full read/write access for our application IAM role. Keep the configuration simple - we'll add versioning and lifecycle rules in the next iteration.
# Category: cloud_storage_security

AWSTemplateFormatVersion: "2010-09-09"
Description: S3 bucket for user-uploaded files and application backups with full read/write access for an application IAM role.

Parameters:
  ApplicationRoleArn:
    Type: String
    Description: ARN of the IAM role used by the application (granted full read/write access to the bucket).
  BucketName:
    Type: String
    Default: ""
    Description: Optional bucket name. Leave blank to let CloudFormation generate one.

Conditions:
  HasBucketName: !Not [!Equals [!Ref BucketName, ""]]

Resources:
  UserUploadsAndBackupsBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !If [HasBucketName, !Ref BucketName, !Ref "AWS::NoValue"]
      PublicAccessBlockConfiguration:
        BlockPublicAcls: true
        BlockPublicPolicy: true
        IgnorePublicAcls: true
        RestrictPublicBuckets: true

  UserUploadsAndBackupsBucketPolicy:
    Type: AWS::S3::BucketPolicy
    Properties:
      Bucket: !Ref UserUploadsAndBackupsBucket
      PolicyDocument:
        Version: "2012-10-17"
        Statement:
          - Sid: AllowAppRoleListBucket
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Ref ApplicationRoleArn
            Action:
              - s3:ListBucket
              - s3:GetBucketLocation
            Resource: !GetAtt UserUploadsAndBackupsBucket.Arn
          - Sid: AllowAppRoleObjectReadWrite
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Ref ApplicationRoleArn
            Action:
              - s3:GetObject
              - s3:GetObjectVersion
              - s3:PutObject
              - s3:DeleteObject
              - s3:DeleteObjectVersion
              - s3:AbortMultipartUpload
              - s3:ListBucketMultipartUploads
              - s3:ListMultipartUploadParts
            Resource: !Sub "${UserUploadsAndBackupsBucket.Arn}/*"

Outputs:
  BucketName:
    Description: Name of the S3 bucket.
    Value: !Ref UserUploadsAndBackupsBucket
  BucketArn:
    Description: ARN of the S3 bucket.
    Value: !GetAtt UserUploadsAndBackupsBucket.Arn
